descript.abstract: This dataset is intended for read-only use. This dataset represents survey monuments as point features. These data can be used to determine the status of corner monuments. Where possible, monuments are place at their ground position. If the ground position is not available, monuments are placed at the calculated survey network location (see Corner dataset).The purpose of these data is to provide display, identification, and analysis tools for determining current boundary information for Forest Service managers, GIS specialists, and others.
descript.purpose: This dataset represents survey monuments as point features. A monument is a permanent marker placed at the location of a corner. Contemporary permanent monuments can be an aluminum pipe and cap or an iron rod and plastic cap. In the past, original Public Land Survey System (PLSS) surveys often utilized wooden stakes or posts, marked trees, or stones, mounds of rocks, pits, or other less-permanent monuments to mark corners.
descript.supplinf: 3 related Business Data Tables with corresponding Relationship Classes: COR_SURVEYEVENT, COR_MONUMENT, COR_CORNERIDENTIFIER.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and land users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

attracc.attraccr: For boundaries within FS Administrative Forests or Proclaimed Forest areas, the Regional Land Status personnel are responsible for their local data stewardship and maintenance. The overall collection nationwide consists of 100% attribute accuracy based on currentness and completeness.

dataqual.logic: Quality control measures taken: this feature class is populated by an automated translation process from the source feature class that is edited and maintained by the data stewards. Only stewards with the proper role grants can edit the data. The application used for editing has built in controls to only allow valid geometry and domains to control attribute integrity.
dataqual.complete: This theme is current to date of publication.

horizpa.horizpar: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from: 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.

procstep.procdesc: This data was published using Feature Manipulation Engine Software. The spatial features were extracted from the transactional database, manipulated to meet the desired output format and published to the target feature class.

enttyp.enttypd: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: A unique control number used to identify a specific spatial feature and/or database attribute in a related object.

edom.edomvd: Monument type is Aluminum Post with a concrete fill
Monument type is Iron Post with a concrete fill
Monument type is not in the standard list
The Monument type attribute is Not Applicable
Monument cap type is not in the standard list

attrdomv.udom: Diameter of monument observed in the unit stored in LENGTHUNIT.
Height of monument observed in the unit stored in LENGTHUNIT.

attr.attrdef: U.S. Forest Service Region, consisting of Region 01 (Northern Region), Region 02 (Rocky Mountain Region), Region 03 (Southwestern Region), Region 04 (Intermountain Region), Region 05 (Pacific Southwest Region), Region 06 (Pacific Northwest Region), Region 08 (Southern Region), Region 09 (Eastern Region), and Region 10 (Alaska Region). There is no Region 07. Regions are listed on https://www.fs.usda.gov/organization. Within this dataset, the region represents the Land Status Record System Production Regional schema from which data was derived.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

distinfo.distliab: The U.S. Forest Service makes no warranty, express or implied, nor assumes any liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness, or utility of these geospatial data or for the improper or incorrect use of those data. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
